messages.content: Fix this RxJS v6 code to v7 idioms: `source$.do(x => console.log(x)).map(x => x * 2).subscribe()`.
Convert this v6 RxJS chain to v7: `obs$.filter(x => x > 0).map(x => x + 1).do(console.log).subscribe()`.
Fix these v6 imports to v7: `import { Observable } from 'rxjs/Observable'; import 'rxjs/add/operator/map';`.
Refactor this v6 snippet to v7: `Observable.fromEvent(input, 'input').debounceTime(300).switchMap(q => search(q))`.
Explain briefly how v6â†’v7 operator imports changed, then show a working typed pipeline.
Convert this v6 migration fragment to v7, no `any`: `source$.catch(err => Observable.of(fallback))`.
Fix this v6 code to v7: `obs$.toPromise().then(v => console.log(v))`.
Refactor this v6 data-layer call to v7 using firstValueFrom: `return http.get(url).toPromise();`.
Explain briefly when to pick lastValueFrom vs firstValueFrom, then show a minimal example.
Fix this v6 async/await code to v7: `const v = await stream$.toPromise();`.
Build a typed helper in RxJS v7 that awaits the first value of an observable with a timeout.
Refactor this v6 code to v7, migrating toPromise to lastValueFrom: `const total = await sum$.toPromise();`.
Write an RxJS v7 function that converts a Promise-returning API into a cold Observable<Result>. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, re-subscription should re-invoke the promise.
Build a Promise â†’ Observable adapter in RxJS v7 using from, and show the pitfall of eager evaluation.
Explain briefly how Promiseâ†’Observable conversion works in RxJS v7, then show a typed implementation that passes an AbortController through.
Fix this v6 conversion code to v7: `Observable.fromPromise(fetch(url))`.
Refactor this Promise chain into an RxJS v7 observable pipeline: `loadUser(id).then(u => loadOrders(u.id)).then(os => render(os))`.
Add a capability: wrap this existing Promise-based API into a cold Observable that retries on failure. Snippet: `async function getData(): Promise<Data> { ... }`.
Explain briefly the hot vs cold observable pitfall, then show a typed example where a cold source is unintentionally replayed twice.
Fix this v6 hot-observable pattern to v7 and avoid the double-subscription pitfall: `obs$.publishReplay(1).refCount()`.
Build a hot Observable<Price> in RxJS v7 that shares a single upstream tick across many consumers.
Refactor this cold observable to avoid the hot/cold pitfall of re-running the HTTP call for each subscriber. Snippet: `const me$ = defer(() => from(fetchMe()));`.
Explain briefly the pitfall of subscribing to a Subject after it has emitted, then show the typed fix with ReplaySubject.
Fix this v6 error-handling fragment to v7: `obs$.catch(err => Observable.of(fallback)).retryWhen(errors => errors.delay(500))`.
Explain briefly when to pick catchError vs retry in RxJS v7, then show a typed example combining both.
Refactor this v5 error recovery code into v7 idioms using retry with exponential backoff: `obs$.retry(3).catch(e => Observable.of(null))`.
Build a fetchJson$ helper in RxJS v7 with 3 retries + exponential backoff, and a typed fallback via catchError.
Fix this v6 snippet to v7: `obs$.retryWhen(errs => errs.scan((acc, e) => acc + 1, 0).delay(1000))`.
Refactor this pipeline to distinguish transient (retry) from fatal (catchError) errors: `obs$.pipe(retry(3), catchError(() => of(null)))`.
Build a typed event bus in RxJS v7 using a Subject and asyncScheduler so observers receive events asynchronously, avoiding re-entrant teardown.
Fix this sync Subject code to avoid re-entrant subscribe-during-emit using asyncScheduler: `const s = new Subject<number>(); s.subscribe(v => s.next(v + 1));`.
Explain briefly why asyncScheduler matters for Subject teardown, then show a minimal typed implementation.
Refactor this eager Subject to use subscribeOn(asyncScheduler) so the producer side runs deferred: `const s = new Subject<Cmd>(); fireEagerly(s);`.
Add a capability: schedule emissions from this Subject onto the asyncScheduler so teardown is safe during event handlers. Snippet: `const events = new Subject<E>();`.
Fix this v6 multicast chain to v7: `source$.multicast(new Subject()).refCount()`.
Refactor this v6 multicast pattern to v7 using shareReplay: `source$.multicast(new ReplaySubject(1)).refCount()`.
Explain briefly how multicast was refactored in v7, then show a typed example using connectable for manual control.
Build an RxJS v7 hot source using connectable and a BehaviorSubject connector for deferred manual connection.
Refactor this v6 publish/connect code to v7 connectable: `const m$ = source$.publish(); m$.subscribe(a); m$.subscribe(b); m$.connect();`.
Fix this v6 share usage in v7: `source$.publishBehavior(initial).refCount()`.
